problem_summary: Weekly review generation and completed workflow revisions can create committed local state without publishing it, leaving the remote repository behind and making the audit trail incomplete outside the current machine.
impact: Weekly review artifacts, SQLite changes, journals, workflow code, tests, and revision audit metadata may remain local even though the workflow appears complete.
proposed_diff: 1. Require every generated weekly journal review and completed weekly skill-improvement review to publish the complete repository state. 2. Add a standard-library git-publish core operation and CLI command that checkpoints SQLite WAL, stages all files, commits when needed, detects the branch/upstream or establishes origin as upstream, and pushes. Treat push failure as incomplete. 3. Make propose-skill-revision publish proposal and audit commits, and make terminal workflow-revision outcomes publish implementation and final audit commits. 4. Update SKILL.md, AGENTS.md, and skill-improvement guidance. 5. Add local bare-remote tests for proposal, weekly-review, and applied-revision publication.
compatibility: Configured upstreams remain unchanged; origin is used only when no upstream exists. Missing remotes, detached HEAD, or rejected pushes fail explicitly. No API client or non-standard dependency is added.
tests: Run the approved conda pytest suite.
Verify proposal, weekly-review, and applied-revision commits reach a local bare remote.
Verify missing remote and detached branch failures are explicit.
token_cost_change: neutral: deterministic Git operations and concise instructions add no semantic-agent context.
